candidateClaimParaphrase: Across the tested bilateral and unilateral seated heel raises with 15 kg on the thigh, modeled peak Achilles tendon force group means ranged from 0.5 to 0.7 BW.
supportedCondition: Tested bilateral and unilateral seated heel raises with 15 kg placed on the thigh; laboratory footwear and force plates; non-fatigued cohort.
measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and ground reaction force; plantarflexion moment divided by a fixed 5 cm moment arm; modeled Achilles tendon force normalized to participant bodyweight.
evidenceLocator: Baxter et al. 2021, Table 1, seated heel raise (2-leg and 1-leg) rows
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required. The 0.5 to 0.7 BW result is a range across two reported group means, not one 0.6 BW group mean; the source used 15 kg thigh loading and a modeled fixed moment arm.

candidateClaimParaphrase: During the tested standing single-leg heel raise, modeled peak Achilles tendon force was 3.0 plus or minus 0.3 BW.
supportedCondition: Tested standing single-leg heel raise at study cadence; bodyweight; laboratory footwear and force plates; non-fatigued cohort.
measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and ground reaction force; plantarflexion moment divided by a fixed 5 cm moment arm; modeled Achilles tendon force normalized to participant bodyweight.
evidenceLocator: Baxter et al. 2021, Table 1, standing heel raise (1-leg) row
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required. Healthy sample of eight; force was modeled with a fixed 5 cm moment arm and study cadence may differ from app prescriptions.

candidateClaimParaphrase: Repeated single-leg forward and lateral hopping produced modeled peak Achilles tendon force group means of 7.3 BW; transfer to the app hop-and-stick exercise is a close variant.
supportedCondition: Tested repeated single-leg forward and lateral hopping; bodyweight; laboratory footwear and force plates; app target adds a stick landing.
measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and ground reaction force; plantarflexion moment divided by a fixed 5 cm moment arm; modeled Achilles tendon force normalized to participant bodyweight.
evidenceLocator: Baxter et al. 2021, Table 1, lateral and forward hopping (1-leg) rows
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required. The source tested repeated hopping rather than the exact hop-and-stick protocol; transfer is explicitly limited to a close variant.

candidateClaimParaphrase: During the tested standing single-leg heel raise, modeled Achilles tendon loading rate was 13.1 plus or minus 3.4 BW per second.
supportedCondition: Tested standing single-leg heel raise at study cadence; bodyweight; laboratory footwear and force plates; non-fatigued cohort.
measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and ground reaction force; plantarflexion moment divided by a fixed 5 cm moment arm; modeled Achilles tendon force normalized to participant bodyweight.
evidenceLocator: Baxter et al. 2021, Table 1, standing heel raise (1-leg) row and loading-rate method
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required. Loading rate is the peak change over a 5% moving window; no independent app-wide loading-rate band threshold is supported.

candidateClaimParaphrase: During the tested double-leg full-depth squat, modeled peak patellar tendon force was 3.4 plus or minus 0.7 BW.
supportedCondition: Tested bodyweight double-leg full-depth squat at study-defined speed and range; laboratory force plates; non-fatigued cohort.
measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and force plates; knee extension moment divided by an angle-specific patellar tendon moment arm; modeled tendon force normalized to participant bodyweight.
evidenceLocator: Scattone Silva et al. 2024, Table 1, 2-leg squat (full) row
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required. Healthy cohort; sagittal-plane constrained model omitted hamstring coactivation and the source tier is a within-study composite index.

measurementMethod: 3D motion capture and force plates; model-estimated quadriceps and patellofemoral joint force; loading index uses 50% normalized peak and 50% normalized impulse.
evidenceLocator: Song et al. 2023, Table 1, double-leg squat (60 degrees) row
candidateNotes: Technically re-audited claim candidate only; explicit human batch approval and formal final-claim promotion remain required.
